response:
# Top 3 risk-reducers for `src/billing.ts`

## 1. Extract `generateInvoice` into named helpers (crime_00001 â€” God Function)
- 204 lines (50â€“253), 3.4Ă— the 60-line domain threshold, severity 0.9.
- This is the single biggest agent-risk surface in the file: it almost certainly mixes pricing, proration, tax, persistence, and side effects, so any agent editing one branch will load incomplete context for the rest.
- It also subsumes other findings: the 7 wall-clock reads (crime_00004) and the 4 TODOs (crime_00020) are scattered through this function (lines 57â€“273 / 183â€“308). Splitting first makes them addressable in isolation, and the file drops below the 300-line God File threshold (crime_00010) as a free side effect.

## 2. Split or rename `calculateRefundPreview` + encode the comment rule (crime_00008 + crime_00011)
- The name suggests a pure preview, but the body calls `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail` (lines 40â€“44). An agent will reuse it expecting no I/O and double-fire an audit/email.
- Directly above it (line 39) sits the un-encoded rule: *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves."* Crimes flags this as Logic in the Alibi because the surrounding code doesn't reference `owner`, `plan`, or `billing` â€” the authorization gate lives only in prose.
- Fix together: extract the pure calculation (`previewRefundAmount`) from the side-effecting `processRefund`, and turn the comment into a named guard (`canRefundEnterprisePlan(actor, plan)`) with a test. Pairing them is what closes the loop â€” renaming alone leaves the rule unenforced.

## 3. Inject a clock to kill Temporal Recklessness (crime_00004)
- 7 direct `Date.now()` / `new Date()` reads at lines 57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273 â€” billing code reading wall time is the textbook case where tests have to monkey-patch `Date` to pin invoice dates, due dates, and proration boundaries.
- Replace with a `Clock` (or `now: () => number`) passed into the module/function. Cheap to do, makes every invoice path deterministic, and removes the "tests couple to wall time" failure mode that `test_gap: 0.5` is already hinting at.

---

Deliberately left out: the `"enterprise"` literal sprinkle (crime_00007) is real but cross-cutting across 4 files â€” better tackled as part of a plan/registry consolidation, not inside billing.ts. And the God File finding (crime_00010) resolves itself once #1 lands.
